page: Microsoft Azure D32d_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The configuration provides 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. Memory capacity stands at 128.0 GB, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 1229 GB local disk, while it lacks any dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data places the D32d_v5 in the average performance tier across most compute and memory workloads. The system achieved a single-core Geekbench score of 1618.0 and a multi-core score of 13741.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 36837.52. Memory bandwidth tests show 1667.63 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 134.84 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. In database operations, the server handled 15078.57 Passmark database operations and 29.76 million Redis SET operations per second.

The instance exhibits distinct performance tradeoffs in machine learning tasks. While it demonstrates strong text generation capabilities for small language models (135M parameters) at 304.37 tokens per second, performance drops significantly for larger models, yielding a poor 2.3 tokens per second on 70B parameter models due to the lack of GPU acceleration. Given its balanced resource allocation and substantial local storage, the D32d_v5 is qualitatively cost-efficient for general-purpose enterprise applications, static web serving, software compilation, and database hosting, but is unsuitable for large-scale AI training or heavy LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D32d_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDv5 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz, delivering 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores. The hardware profile includes 128.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 1229 GB local disk, with no GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across standard compute and memory tasks, with a Passmark CPU score of 36837.52 and a Redis SET throughput of 29.76 million operations per second. While the instance shows strong performance in text generation for small 135M parameter models, it exhibits poor throughput for large 70B models. This server is best suited for balanced workloads such as database hosting, software compilation, static web serving, and general enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32d_v5 is a general-purpose VM featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 1229 GB local disk. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, it is ideal for database hosting and general enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32d_v5 is an Intel-powered general-purpose VM with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, substantial local storage, and reliable general-purpose compute performance.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
128.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
Bundled 1229 GB local storage disk for fast data access
Strong text generation performance for small-scale 135M parameter models
Well-suited for database hosting, static web serving, and software compilation
